name: Maersk Ocean Freight & Logistics API
what_it_does: Maersk Developer API for ocean freight and integrated logistics platform. Enables AI agents to manage container booking and space reservation workflows, handle shipment tracking and real-time container location, access schedule and port call data for vessel routing, retrieve Bill of Lading (B/L) and documentation management, manage spot rate quotation and booking confirmation, handle customs and trade documentation filing, access cargo tracking milestones and ETD/ETA data, retrieve emissions and carbon footprint reporting, manage inland transport and last-mile delivery integration, and integrate ocean freight data with TMS, ERP, and supply chain visibility platforms.
use_cases: Managing container bookings and space reservations from logistics automation agents
Tracking container shipments and ETAs from supply chain visibility agents
Accessing spot rate quotes from freight procurement agents
Integrating ocean freight data with TMS and ERP from supply chain automation agents
not_for: Domestic-only road and rail freight without ocean container shipping
Air freight without ocean container logistics context
Small parcel shipping without FCL/LCL container focus
best_when: An importer, exporter, 3PL, or freight forwarder using Maersk wants AI agents to automate container booking, shipment tracking, rate management, documentation, and supply chain visibility integration.
avoid_when: OPERATIONAL RISK: Container booking automation creates binding freight contracts — booking errors and cancellations incur significant fees. Real-time container tracking data has delays and gaps for ocean voyages. Customs documentation automation must be accurate — errors cause shipment delays, demurrage, and penalties. Dangerous goods booking requires human verification.

auth.notes: Maersk uses OAuth 2.0 for Developer API access. Consumer key/secret credentials with booking, tracking, and schedule scopes. Maersk Developer Portal with API documentation and sandbox. Webhooks for container tracking and booking events. Ocean API (DCSA-compliant standards for container tracking).

pricing.free_tier_limits: Developer sandbox available; production access requires Maersk customer relationship
pricing.paid_tiers: API access tied to commercial freight relationship; no separate API pricing
pricing.notes: Copenhagen, Denmark. A.P. Moller - Maersk. Founded 1904. World's largest container shipping company. 17% global container market share. 700+ vessels. $81.5B annual revenue (2022 peak). Maersk is transforming from shipping line to integrated logistics company. DCSA founding member for industry API standards. Competes with MSC and CMA CGM for ocean freight.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth application; tracking API throttled

known_agent_gotchas: OPERATIONAL RISK: Container booking automation creates binding freight contracts — cancellation fees can be significant
Ocean tracking gaps — container location is updated at port calls (every 1-3 weeks); not real-time GPS
No public MCP server — OAuth2 REST API with Maersk Developer Portal
DCSA-compliant tracking API follows industry standard but carrier implementation varies — verify compatibility
Production access requires Maersk customer relationship — sandbox API does not guarantee production access
Dangerous goods (IMO) booking requires human verification — automated DG booking creates safety risk
agent_readiness.security_notes: Ocean freight and shipping data. SOC2, ISO27001, GDPR. OAuth2. EU/US/APAC data residency. Freight contract and customs documentation sensitivity.

security_score_components.security_notes: Ocean freight and logistics data. SOC2, ISO27001, GDPR. OAuth2. Global data residency. Freight contract and trade document sensitivity.
